name: Montemayor, Patricia
evidence: Cannot identify. Public records confirm a Patricia (Ojeda) Montemayor resident in Laredo TX 78045, but nothing ties her to the filed 'VP'/self-employed occupation, to any business, or to George Morales III / Travis County. Montemayor is a very common Laredo surname and no Laredo-to-Austin business, family, or political link was found.

name: Garza, Rudy
evidence: Cannot identify: no zip, occupation, or employer on file, and at least five distinct real Austin-area Rudy Garzas exist (GarzaEMC engineering president and ex-Austin assistant city manager; G-51 Capital VC founder; Rudy D. Garza of CPS Energy/LCRA; Del Mar College regent; an Austin anesthesiologist). The GarzaEMC figure best fits the donation pattern but no hard link was found — no Rudy Garza appears in the City of Austin lobbyist registry. The 219-donation FEC name-match almost certainly aggregates several of these people and should not be trusted.

name: Hernandez, Robert
evidence: Cannot identify. Established only what the filing already states: a Robert Hernandez in Austin 78723 gave $1,030 to Brigid Shea across 2021-2023 with no occupation or employer disclosed. Texas Ethics Commission lists, Austin/Travis County board rosters, and LinkedIn surfaced only non-matching Robert Hernandezes (an Austin Telco FCU president/CEO with no 78723 or donation link, a Dallas business owner, a photographer).

name: Duncan, John
evidence: Cannot identify which John Duncan. The pattern is consistent with a genuine constituent — 6 recurring gifts totaling $1,030 to Ann Howard 2022-2025, and Pct 3 covers 78738 (Bee Cave/Lakeway/Spanish Oaks) — but no employer or occupation is on file and no John Duncan in 78738 surfaced in LinkedIn, Austin Business Journal, Lakeway/Bee Cave rosters, or TEC records.

name: Jane E. Sanders
evidence: Cannot identify. No local Austin/Travis County Jane E. Sanders located in news, TEC/Travis County filings, or professional sources. Explicitly rejecting the Bernie Sanders' spouse hypothesis: nothing ties her to a Travis County commissioner race and a $1,000 gift to a local Texas county race would be highly anomalous. The 320 name-only FEC matches (lean 0.77) are near-certainly an aggregation of many distinct people.

name: Coleman, Charles
evidence: Cannot identify. Extremely common name with no zip or occupation on file. The well-known Charles F. Coleman Jr. (civil rights attorney) is Brooklyn-based with no Austin or Travillion connection; the sole Texas bar hit, Charles E. Coleman, was licensed in 1949 with no Austin locality. The 700 name-only FEC matches aggregate many people.

name: Schwertner, James
resolved_employer: Schwertner Farms, Inc. dba Capitol Land & Livestock — President/CEO (probable)
evidence: Probably Jim (James) Schwertner, President/CEO of Schwertner Farms Inc. dba Capitol Land & Livestock (one of the largest US cattle dealers, Williamson County) and Chairman of Schwertner State Bank; Texas State Directory lists him as 'Jim Schwertner' of Austin, matching the filing's 'Austin, TX', and the surname is rare and tightly tied to this Williamson County family. NOT confirmed: the 78641/Leander residence, and whether he gives to Democrats (his 15 FEC gifts lean 0.0/all-Republican, so the $1,000 to Democrat Ann Howard is an outlier). Industry left null: his core business is cattle trading/ranching and the taxonomy has no Agriculture label — 'Finance' (bank chairmanship) or 'Transportation' would each capture only a secondary role.

name: Porter, Daniel

Austin-area and Ann Howard campaign coverage returned nothing; the only Texas 'Porter' megadonors surfaced were Michael and Mary Porter (Hill Country GOP), a different name. name: Molina, Rene
evidence: Cannot identify. No FEC, news, LinkedIn, obituary, or state-filing record ties a Rene Molina in Houston 77041 to any prior career; his federal records list only 'Ret'. Two other Texas Rene Molinas surfaced (a stucco/masonry owner in 77449, an unrelated profile) but neither matches zip 77041 nor the retiree Democratic-donation pattern.

name: Harper, Alan
resolved_employer: Lenders & Members Service Group, Inc. (Arlington, TX) — President (probable)
evidence: FEC 2012 itemizations show 'ALAN HARPER (LENDERS & MEMBERS SERVICES, INC./PRESIDENT), zip 76016' giving $2,500 to the Republican Party of Texas — exact zip match to this donor — plus $300 to the RNC and $250 to AOPA PAC under 'L & M Service Group Inc./President of Financial Services' (76015, business zip). The heavily Republican federal pattern matches this donor's 27 gifts / 0.057 lean; the $1,000 to Democrat Jeff Travillion remains an unexplained outlier. The firm is a small Arlington financial/tax services company (Stanley K. Harper, CEO), apparently family-run.
